attributes.description: Alerts when average operation time per op exceeds threshold by operation type. Identifies slow operations.
attributes.name: [MongoDB OTel] High operation latency

investigation_guide.blob:
## MongoDB high operation latency

### What fired
Per-operation-type latency rose above the computed threshold.

### Why it matters
Rising average operation latency indicates server- or storage-side slowdowns. Break down by operation type (insert/query/update/command) to localise.

### Triage
1. Check disk I/O and WT cache hit.
2. Look for slow queries in profiler logs.

### Remediation
- Fix slow queries / missing indexes.
- Scale storage.

### Tuning
- Thresholds tune in the query.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-mongodbreceiver.otel-default
// Scope to MongoDB OTel metrics
| WHERE data_stream.dataset == "mongodbreceiver.otel"
| WHERE metrics.mongodb.operation.time IS NOT NULL OR metrics.mongodb.operation.count IS NOT NULL
// operation.time and operation.count are in separate docs; FORK to combine
| FORK (
    WHERE metrics.mongodb.operation.time IS NOT NULL
    | STATS op_time_rate = SUM(RATE(metrics.mongodb.operation.time)) BY resource.attributes.server.address, attributes.operation
    | EVAL server = resource.attributes.server.address, op = attributes.operation, op_count_rate = 0.0
  )
  (
    WHERE metrics.mongodb.operation.count IS NOT NULL
    | STATS op_count_rate = SUM(RATE(metrics.mongodb.operation.count)) BY resource.attributes.server.address, attributes.operation
    | EVAL server = resource.attributes.server.address, op = attributes.operation, op_time_rate = 0.0
  )
| STATS op_time_rate = SUM(op_time_rate), op_count_rate = SUM(op_count_rate) BY server, op
| WHERE op_count_rate > 0
| EVAL avg_latency_ms = ROUND(op_time_rate / op_count_rate, 2)
// Alert when avg latency exceeds 100ms (adjust for your slow query threshold)
| WHERE avg_latency_ms > 100
| SORT avg_latency_ms DESC
| LIMIT 20
